routes.name: The Route 66 Classic
routes.theme: Follow the Mother Road through the Mojave and across Arizona to Colorado

highlights.name: Paso Robles Wine Country
highlights.description: Central Coast wine town with dog-friendly patio tastings and rolling vineyard views

highlights.name: Tehachapi Loop Railroad
highlights.description: National Historic Landmark where freight trains spiral over themselves — viewable from roadside pulloff

highlights.name: Calico Ghost Town
highlights.description: Restored 1880s silver mining town with mine tours and boardwalk strolling — dogs welcome on leash

lodging.name: La Quinta Inn & Suites Barstow
lodging.dog_policy: Dogs welcome free of charge, no size limit, up to 2 pets

lodging.name: Holiday Inn Express Barstow
lodging.dog_policy: Pets allowed with $25/night fee, up to 50 lbs

lodging.name: Desert Hills Motor Hotel
lodging.dog_policy: Classic Route 66 motel, dogs welcome with $15 fee

highlights.name: Route 66 Seligman
highlights.description: Birthplace of Route 66 preservation — vintage diners, neon signs, and the inspiration for Pixar's Cars

highlights.name: Snow Cap Drive-In Seligman
highlights.description: Legendary roadside stop with joke-pulling staff and kitschy Route 66 folk art everywhere

camping.name: Bonito Campground

highlights.name: Sunset Crater Lava Flow Trail
highlights.description: Walk across a 1,000-year-old lava flow with interpretive signs — dogs on leash allowed on paved trail

highlights.name: Flagstaff Urban Trail System
highlights.description: 50+ miles of interconnected trails through ponderosa forest right from town — all dog-friendly

highlights.name: Mother Road Brewing Company

highlights.name: Petrified Forest National Park

lodging.name: La Quinta Inn & Suites Albuquerque Journal Center
lodging.dog_policy: Dogs welcome free, no size limit

lodging.name: Hotel Albuquerque at Old Town
lodging.dog_policy: Pets up to 50 lbs, $50 fee per stay, walking distance to Old Town plaza

lodging.dog_policy: Pets allowed with $35 fee per stay
